description: Evaluations for the Polymarket Analyst skill

evals.prompt: Analyze the Polymarket trading account 0x4f2f6e62c2be5e8c28b5e5d5b5c5a5b5c5d5e5f0. I want to understand how they make money and what their trading strategy is.
evals.expected_output: A structured analyst report with performance metrics table, market concentration analysis, timing patterns, and a strategy attribution with confidence level.
assertions: The output includes a performance metrics table with at least: Total Realized P&L, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Avg Position Size
The output includes a strategy attribution section naming one of the six canonical strategies
The output includes a market concentration section identifying top trading categories
The output mentions running fetch_trader_data.py or making API calls to data-api.polymarket.com
The output includes a confidence level (High/Medium/Low) for the strategy attribution
The output notes any data limitations or caveats

evals.prompt: Who are the top 10 Polymarket traders by all-time profit? For each one, tell me their estimated trading strategy.
evals.expected_output: A leaderboard analysis showing top traders with their P&L figures and strategy classifications, based on fetched data.
assertions: The output fetches leaderboard data (mentions leaderboard endpoint or fetch script with --leaderboard flag)
The output lists at least 5 traders with their P&L figures
The output assigns a strategy label to each major trader
The output includes a comparison table or structured list
The output acknowledges that full strategy analysis requires per-wallet data fetch

evals.prompt: Compare these two Polymarket wallets and tell me which has a better risk-adjusted return and why: 0xabc123... and 0xdef456...
evals.expected_output: A side-by-side comparison of both wallets with key metrics, Sharpe ratio comparison, drawdown comparison, and a clear recommendation.
assertions: The output fetches data for both wallets
The output includes a side-by-side comparison table
The output computes or discusses risk-adjusted metrics (Sharpe, drawdown)
The output identifies which trader has a better risk profile and explains why
The output identifies different strategy types for each wallet if applicable

evals.prompt: I want to copy trade on Polymarket. Find me accounts that use a domain specialist strategy in political markets and have made over $100k.
evals.expected_output: A filtered list of traders matching the criteria with evidence supporting the domain specialist classification.
assertions: The output fetches leaderboard data to identify high-profit traders
The output filters or describes filtering for political market specialists
The output uses herfindahl index or category concentration to justify classification
The output includes win rates specifically in political markets
The output includes a caveat about copy trading risks

evals.prompt: Is this Polymarket trader 0x789abc... using insider information? Their win rate seems suspiciously high.
evals.expected_output: A forensic timing analysis with the insider_timing_flag assessment, entry lag analysis, and a balanced conclusion.
assertions: The output fetches and analyzes timing data for the wallet
The output computes or estimates entry lag vs market creation
The output checks the news_correlation or timing patterns
The output presents the insider_timing_flag assessment
The output presents a balanced conclusion that neither confirms nor denies insider trading
The output notes that on-chain data alone cannot prove intent
